name: Guadalhorce and Guadalteba reservoirs - circular via viewpoints
name_original: Embalses de Guadalhorce y Guadalteba – circular por miradores
region_intro: Hiking around Campillos, located in the Guadalteba region of Málaga province, Spain, offers diverse natural landscapes for outdoor activities. The area features extensive wetlands, including a complex of eleven lagoons, alongside agricultural fields and olive groves. Portions of the Guadalhorce and Guadalteba reservoirs also extend into the municipality, providing additional waterside terrain. This varied geography supports a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Campillos The most popular hiking route is Guadalhorce and Guadalteba reservoirs - circular via viewpoints, an easy 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 19 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views of the surrounding reservoirs. Another top favourite among local hikers is Three Reservoirs Viewpoint – Viewpoint of the Three Reservoirs loop from Mirador de los Tres Embalses, an easy 2.3 miles (3.8 km) path. This trail provides scenic views of the interconnected reservoir system. Local hikers also love the View of Guadalhorce Reservoir loop from Río Turón, a 4.6 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through the landscape surrounding the Guadalhorce Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Hiking around Campillos is defined by its significant wetlands, expansive reservoirs, and agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks around lagoons to moderate trails near the reservoirs. The routes in Campillos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 1600 reviews. More than 15000 hikers have used komoot to explore Campillos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Campillos?
A: There are over 18 hiking trails in and around Campillos, offering a variety of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. These routes are well-regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking trails in Campillos?
A: The hiking trails around Campillos primarily range from easy to moderate. You'll find 10 easy routes, perfect for a relaxed stroll, and 8 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge. There are no difficult routes listed in this guide.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Campillos?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Campillos are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995631591/guadalhorce-and-guadalteba-reservoirs-circular-via-viewpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalhorce and Guadalteba reservoirs - circular via viewpoints&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.6 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34352038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Reservoirs Viewpoint – Viewpoint of the Three Reservoirs loop from Mirador de los Tres Embalses&lt;/a&gt; is another popular easy circular option.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Campillos?
A: Campillos is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, ideal for exploring the wetlands and reservoirs. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to hike, though some trails might be wetter. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended if visiting during this season.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Campillos?
A: Absolutely. Many of the easy routes around Campillos are suitable for families. The trails around the Lagunas de Campillos Nature Reserve are particularly good for all ages, offering flat terrain and excellent birdwatching opportunities. The shorter, easy loops around the reservoirs, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995631591/guadalhorce-and-guadalteba-reservoirs-circular-via-viewpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalhorce and Guadalteba reservoirs - circular via viewpoints&lt;/a&gt;, are also great choices for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Campillos?
A: Many trails in natural areas around Campillos are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is kept on a leash. However, specific rules can vary, particularly within protected areas like the Lagunas de Campillos Nature Reserve, where wildlife conservation is a priority. It's always best to check local signage or park regulations for the specific trail you plan to hike.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while hiking near Campillos?
A: Hiking near Campillos offers diverse natural beauty. You can explore the extensive Lagunas de Campillos Nature Reserve, a complex of eleven lagoons perfect for birdwatching. The region also features parts of the Guadalhorce and Guadalteba reservoirs, providing scenic lakeside trails. For a more dramatic experience, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey&lt;/a&gt; offers breathtaking views through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles to explore near Campillos?
A: Yes, you can combine hiking with historical exploration. The region has valuable archaeological sites, including Roman village ruins in Guadalteba Park and the 4,000-year-old Necropolis of Aguilillas. For those interested in castles, you can find nearby historical structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1121683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Peña de Ardales&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8221970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Star&lt;/a&gt;, which can be integrated into your hiking plans.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Campillos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1600 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, particularly the serene beauty of the wetlands and reservoirs, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels. The opportunity for birdwatching and the peaceful atmosphere are frequently highlighted.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads around Campillos?
A: While Campillos itself has bus connections, direct public transport to specific remote trailheads might be limited. For trails within the Lagunas de Campillos Nature Reserve or around the main reservoirs, driving is often the most convenient option. For popular attractions like El Caminito del Rey, dedicated shuttle services or public buses from nearby towns like Ardales or El Chorro are available, but it's advisable to check schedules in advance.

Q: Are there specific parking facilities for hikers at the trailheads?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes around the reservoirs, you'll often find informal parking areas or designated spots near viewpoints and access points. For the Lagunas de Campillos, there are usually small parking areas. When visiting major attractions like El Caminito del Rey, there are official parking lots, often with a fee, and it's recommended to arrive early, especially during peak season.

Q: What is the famous El Caminito del Rey, and how close is it to Campillos?
A: El Caminito del Rey is a spectacular pathway through cliffs and canyons, known for its wooden walkways and glass-floored platforms. It traverses the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking views. Campillos is strategically located, making it an excellent base to access El Caminito del Rey, which is a short drive away. It's approximately 7.7 km long and requires advance booking.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the hiking trails in Campillos?
A: Yes, many trails, especially those around the reservoirs, offer stunning viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995631591/guadalhorce-and-guadalteba-reservoirs-circular-via-viewpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalhorce and Guadalteba reservoirs - circular via viewpoints&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34352038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Reservoirs Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another popular spot for scenic views of the interconnected reservoir system.
tips: This bridge forms the end of Stage 19 of the GR 249 (Gran Senda de Málaga) and is, at the same time, the northern access to the Gaitanes gorge, better known as the Caminito del Rey. From this bridge you can see the Guadalhorce and Guadalteba reservoirs.

After crossing a dense pine forest, a path leads to this clearing where you can get a good view of the Guadalhorce reservoir. On clear days, the color of the water is a turquoise blue that is surprising for its intensity.

waypoints.name: View of the Guadalhorce and Guadalteba reservoirs
